question_text: A student plots a force-extension graph for a spring and finds that when 15 N is applied, the spring extends 0.3 m. What is the spring constant of this spring in N/m?
explanation: Using F = ke, we rearrange to k = F/e. So k = 15 N ÷ 0.3 m = 50 N/m. A higher spring constant means the spring is stiffer and harder to stretch. The N/m units show how many newtons of force are needed to extend the spring by 1 metre.
